commands.review_summary:
The PR changes SQLite WAL maintenance so periodic checkpoints default to PASSIVE while explicit and close-time checkpoints retain TRUNCATE, with regression tests for defaults and overrides.

Reproducibility: no. for a full live stall reproduction in this review; yes at source level because current main schedules a synchronous TRUNCATE checkpoint every 30 minutes through DatabaseSync.exec, and the linked issue provides matching idle-stall logs.
commands.review_followup: No repair job is needed because the PR has no blocking review findings and should proceed through normal review and merge gates.

target.title: fix: use passive periodic sqlite wal checkpoints
target.body:
## Summary
- Make the default background SQLite WAL maintenance checkpoint use `PASSIVE` instead of `TRUNCATE` so the 30-minute timer does less blocking work on the main event loop.
- Keep explicit/manual checkpoints and `close()` on the existing `TRUNCATE` default so WAL cleanup still happens at controlled shutdown/manual boundaries.
- Add regression coverage for the default periodic-vs-close behavior and for explicit `checkpointMode` overrides.

Fixes #81715

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** SQLite WAL maintenance no longer runs the default 30-minute background checkpoint as `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE);`; the periodic path now uses `PASSIVE`, while explicit/close checkpoints keep the existing `TRUNCATE` default.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node `v23.11.0` and pnpm `11.1.0`, including the `src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts` unit contract and a live `node:sqlite` `DatabaseSync` smoke run against a temporary on-disk SQLite database.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `pnpm exec vitest --run src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:changed`
  - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with a temporary `DatabaseSync`, `configureSqliteWalMaintenance(db, { checkpointIntervalMs: 0 })`, one insert, `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE);`, and `maintenance.close()`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture after the patch:

  ```text
  RUN  v4.1.6 /private/tmp/openclaw-wal-81715

   ✓ infra ../../src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts > sqlite WAL maintenance > enables WAL mode and explicit autocheckpointing
   ✓ infra ../../src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts > sqlite WAL maintenance > runs lightweight periodic PASSIVE checkpoints and TRUNCATE on close
   ✓ infra ../../src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts > sqlite WAL maintenance > honors explicit checkpoint mode overrides for periodic and close checkpoints
   ✓ infra ../../src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts > sqlite WAL maintenance > reports checkpoint errors without throwing from background maintenance

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  4 passed (4)
  ```

  Live `node:sqlite` smoke output from the same checkout:

  ```json
  {
    "journal_mode": "wal",
    "passiveCheckpointRows": 1,
    "closeCheckpoint": true
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The regression test observes the timer firing `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE);` by default and `maintenance.close()` firing `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE);`; override coverage shows callers that set `checkpointMode` still get that explicit mode for periodic and close checkpoints. The live SQLite smoke confirmed WAL mode and a successful PASSIVE checkpoint/close checkpoint path on a real temporary database.
- **What was not tested:** A long-running 30-minute production stall was not reproduced; the timer behavior is verified with fake timers and the SQLite runtime path is smoke-tested with a short-lived local database.

## Tests
- `pnpm exec vitest --run src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`
